description: Proof DAG registry index for the Kakeya / restriction program (Wang–Zahl resolution and Guth's survey).

2502.17655.title: Volume estimates for unions of convex sets, and the Kakeya set conjecture
2502.17655.note: Headline (intro-level / navigation granularity: the Assertions D/E induction spine, 10 of 133 math envs). 1 cross-DAG edge (addresses_same_question) to the shared multilinear-Kakeya node.

2604.03416.title: The Kakeya conjecture, after Wang and Zahl
2604.03416.note: Survey / proof-map (sticky $\to$ grain $\to$ complex-conjugation $\to$ sum-product contradiction; non-sticky high-density reduction). 1 cross-DAG edge (addresses_same_question) to the shared multilinear-Kakeya node.

2402.03470.title: A Study Guide for "A Restriction Estimate using Polynomial Partitioning"
2402.03470.note: Restriction member (exposition of Guth's R^3 restriction estimate via polynomial partitioning). References multilinear Kakeya as method lineage (thm:1.1 references math/0509262:thm:1.15); this exposition proves its transversal estimate via Wongkew + polynomial partitioning, so it is in-cluster but not a load-bearing dependency.

1409.4683.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality
1409.4683.note: SHARED node — per-paper DAG canonically in the bourgain-demeter-decoupling registry. A multilinear-Kakeya paper (re_derives BCT thm:1.15, and re_derives Guth's own endpoint result 0811.2251:thm:2 by a shorter argument); thickens the load-bearing hub of the harmonic-analysis region.

0811.2251.title: The endpoint case of the Bennett-Carbery-Tao multilinear Kakeya conjecture
0811.2251.note: SHARED node — per-paper DAG canonically in the bourgain-demeter-decoupling registry. Guth's original endpoint proof of multilinear Kakeya via the polynomial ham-sandwich theorem; improves BCT thm:1.15 (removes the $\epsilon$-loss). Turns the hub into a genuine dependency cluster: BCT ($\epsilon$-loss) $\leftarrow$ Guth endpoint (improves) $\leftarrow$ Guth short proof (re_derives), plus the decoupling-side import on the other face of the hub.

math/0509262.title: On the Multilinear Restriction and Kakeya conjectures
math/0509262.note: SHARED node and hub — the per-paper DAG lives in the bourgain-demeter-decoupling registry (bourgain-demeter-decoupling/papers/math-0509262.json). The load-bearing dependency is on the decoupling side: the $\ell^2$ decoupling theorem is proved from it (decoupling DAG, 1403.5335:thm:6.1 imports_result thm:1.16). Within this DAG: Guth's short proof re-derives it (1409.4683), Guth's restriction references it as method lineage (2402.03470), and Wang–Zahl complete the full conjecture independently (thematic, thm:1.15).
